Empires & Puzzles Penolite Review: The Relentless Gargoyle Strategy Guide

Penolite was once the terror of the Christmas defenses, shredding minions and fiends alike. But with stats from 2021, can this stone sentinel still crack the modern armor of today's meta? We break down the math, the mechanic, and the verdict.
Penolite Review: The Stone-Cold Exorcist vs The Minion Hordes
Penolite, The Stone-Cold Exorcist: Minion Slayer & Healing Nightmare
Penolite, the Relentless Gargoyle, arrived during the Black Friday 2021 event as a direct answer to the overwhelming "minion and fiend" meta that was dominating

at the time (think Bera, Freya, and Elizabeth). As a 5-star Holy Ranger from the Gargoyle family, she brings a unique mechanics-first approach to damage. While she is a "Classic" event hero by today's standards, her specific hatred for summoners gives her a niche that time hasn't completely eroded, even if her stats have gathered a bit of moss.

Penolite’s design is strikingly gothic and eerie, fitting perfectly with the Gargoyle theme. She features a stone-like complexion, large wings, and a tail that hints at her non-human nature. The crossbow she wields is massive, suggesting heavy artillery rather than precise sniping. It’s a cool, stoic design that screams "I am here to do a job, not to make friends," which is ironic considering her job is specifically killing the "friends" (minions) your enemies summon.
When looking at Penolite's stats in the harsh light of 2026, the power creep is undeniable.
Once considered high, this is now significantly lower than modern hitters who regularly push 1300+ base attack.
These defensive stats are arguably her biggest liability. Modern snipers can often one-shot her before her Stoneskin even activates.
. She relies entirely on her Stoneskin passive and Limit Breaks to survive in today's environment.
This skill is the definition of "situational brilliance."
This is negligible in the current meta. It’s a "tickle" to modern tanks with 3000 HP. Do not bring her to a fight if the enemy has no summoners.
This is where she shines. Dealing 490% damage to
enemies is a multiplier usually reserved for slow mana heroes or single-target snipers. If she hits a team of 5 summoners, the total damage output is astronomical.
A -50% reduction in healing is a potent soft-counter to healers, making it difficult for the enemy to recover from her massive strike.
Let's break down the damage potential. We'll assume a Limit Broken (LB1) Attack of ~1000 for easy math, compared to a modern enemy Defense of ~1300.
Average (10 tiles). With a lvl 23 Mana Troop (or lvl 11 Styx/Magic), she charges in 9 tiles. This is crucial for her usability.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Gargoyle family brings one of the strongest defensive passives in the game, which helps mitigate her aging stats.

The Poison/Burn immunity is a nice bonus, especially against the myriad of DoT heroes (like the classic Gravemaker or modern DoT monsters), but it requires fielding multiple Gargoyles, which is less common in mixed modern teams.
It scales infinitely. Whether it's a 300 damage slash or a 5000 damage sniper shot from a shiny new hero, it becomes
It wears off after 4 hits. Minions, DoT ticks, and multi-hitters (like the Construct family) strip this protection instantly.
is the correct Aether Power for her. She is a damage dealer; she needs every point of attack to penetrate the high defense of modern heroes.

Upon release, Penolite was an "Instant Win" button against the Christmas family (Mother North, Santa, Krampus) and the "Fiend" meta (Elizabeth). She forced players to rethink defense teams that relied heavily on minions.

The game has moved to "Mega Minions" and raw stats that dwarf Penolite's. While she still technically counters summoners, the "summoners" she faces now (like the Owl Tower heroes or Construct heroes) often have passives or stats that allow them to survive her hit and kill her in return.

. She is no longer a general-purpose hero. She is a "silver bullet" you keep in your roster specifically for wars where the enemy is running heavy minion/fiend teams (like Undead Horde wars or specific defenses). In top-tier play, she struggles to stay alive long enough to fire.
As of this review, Penolite does not have a costume.
This means she is stuck with her base stats and skills, putting her at a further disadvantage compared to heroes who have received multiple costume buffs.
There is only one valid path for Penolite. You are bringing her to kill things. If she doesn't kill them, she fails.
Maximizes the 490% damage multiplier. Every +15 Attack node translates to significant extra damage on her special.
She remains squishy, but the Stoneskin passive is her primary survival tool, not her HP pool.
Might survive one extra slash attack.
Her base damage (245%) becomes even more laughable, and her "big hit" loses its threat level.
path is mandatory. You want her to hit as hard as possible when she finally charges.
Dealing nearly 500% damage to 5 enemies is one of the highest potential damage outputs in the game.
The -50% healing generation is excellent for crippling stall teams.
Stoneskin allows her to tank sniper shots that would otherwise obliterate her.
Ranger class allows her to bypass defensive buffs (like Riposte or Defense Up), ensuring her damage connects.
If the enemy doesn't have a summoner, she is a "wet noodle." 245% damage at Average speed is D-tier performance.
She is fragile and slow compared to 2025/2026 heroes.
In a world of Very Fast/Charge heroes, 9/10 tiles can feel like an eternity.
Stoneskin is easily stripped by minion hits or damage-over-time ticks.

, the war rule minions usually don't count as "Summoners" for the extra damage trigger (unless the hero itself has a summoning skill). Stick to countering heroes like Freya, Bera, Miriam & Midnight, etc.

Grimble eats the minions to give mana, allowing Penolite to charge faster and wipe the team.
Rhys boosts tiles and attack, Ogima destroys minions, and Penolite cleans up anyone left standing.

If you pulled Penolite early in your career:

She will carry you through map stages and raids where opponents rely on older minion heroes. Max her, emblem her for attack, and pair her with a mana booster if possible. She will be your "problem solver" for annoying summoner teams.

Penolite is a situational tool. Do not put her on defense; she is too easy to play around. On offense, she is worth keeping for War depth, but think twice before using Alpha Aethers (LB2) on her unless you constantly struggle against minion teams. There are better, more versatile Holy heroes for your resources.
The community's relationship with Penolite has been a roller coaster.
"Op heroes like this are killing the game sadly." Players were terrified of the 490% damage potential.
There was a huge debate on whether Costumed Kadilen counted as a "summoner" (since her dodge minions are a passive effect of the buff). It was confirmed she
take the extra damage, making Penolite a favorite counter to the "C.Kadilen Dodge Meta."
"She might just be late to the party." Many players now feel she is too slow and fragile, often dying before she can punish the summoners she was built to destroy.

Penolite is like a gargoyle on a cathedral: impressive, stony, and built for a specific purpose. When the conditions are right (a board full of summoners), she wakes up and rains destruction. When the conditions are wrong, she's just a statue. In the modern era of
, she isn't the meta-defining monster she once was, but she remains a potent ace up your sleeve for those specific, minion-infested battles.
Verdict: Keep her benched until you see a minion, then unleash the stone beast.
Is Penolite still the ultimate counter to the minion meta in Empires & Puzzles? We analyze her stats, special skill, and modern viability in this comprehensive review.
